Title: Chromosome-level assembly and analysis of the Thymus genome provide insights into glandular secretory trichome formation and monoterpenoid biosynthesis in thyme

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase (TPS); contains DDXXD, NSE/DTE, and RRX8W motifs
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid biosynthesis; plastidial MEP-derived terpene pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phenolic monoterpene precursor branch leading to thymol/carvacrol via γ-terpinene
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: γ-terpinene (main product); also β-thujene, sabinene, and terpinolene as by-products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranyl diphosphate to predominantly γ-terpinene and minor monoterpene by-products
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of thymol and carvacrol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mainly flowers and leaves; highest VOCs in buds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial and antioxidant bioactivity; medicinal and aromatic value
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpression module MEindianred3; identified as DEG in thyme tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with tissues rich in volatile monoterpenes; expression supported by transcriptome and WGCNA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes production of γ-terpinene, the major monoterpene precursor for thymol and carvacrol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tq02G002290.1 clustered phylogenetically with known γ-terpinene synthases from oregano and thyme. 2. Recombinant enzyme converted GPP mainly into γ-terpinene in vitro. 3. The enzyme also produced minor by-products β-thujene, sabinene, and terpinolene. 4. The gene was therefore named TqTPS1 and identified as a key monoterpene biosynthetic gene in thyme.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; transcriptome/WGCNA coexpression; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli BL21; purified recombinant protein in vitro enzyme assay with GPP; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for monoterpene skeleton formation; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stage and organ type; VOCs highest in flower buds and higher in flowers than leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note TqTPS1 is a versatile enzyme producing multiple products; whether it is a multisubstrate enzyme remains undetermined.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly: NCBI PRJNA690675; China National Genomics Data Center GWHBJVA00000000; RNA-seq transcriptomics; metabolomics/volatile profiling by HS-SPME-GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for metabolic engineering or marker-assisted breeding to increase γ-terpinene-derived phenolic monoterpenes such as thymol and carvacrol; potentially useful in gene stacking with downstream CYP71D and SDR genes

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted to cyclize GPP to γ-terpinene based on phylogeny
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Likely forms precursor of thymol and carvacrol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate positive contributor to γ-terpinene-derived monoterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Phylogenetic analysis suggested Tq13G005250.1 may encode a γ-terpinene synthase. 2. The gene grouped with monoterpenoid-associated TPS genes and co-occurred in a terpenoid-associated WGCNA module. 3. No direct biochemical assay was reported for this candidate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; transcriptome differential expression; WGCNA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Organ and floral developmental stage differences in volatile accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted candidate only; not functionally validated biochemically in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: NCBI PRJNA690675; GWHBJVA00000000; RNA-seq; volatile metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for future functional validation and gene stacking with TqTPS1, CYP71D, and SDR genes

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream precursor supply for monoterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for monoterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expanded DXS copy number likely enhances precursor supply for monoterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Four DXS DEGs were identified in the T. quinquecostatus genome. 2. DXS copy number was expanded in thyme. 3. Authors explicitly describe DXS as the crucial rate-limiting enzyme of the MEP pathway, supporting increased monoterpenoid precursor supply.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome annotation; DEG analysis; pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Crucial rate-limiting enzyme of the MEP pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study discusses the family-level role rather than validating a single DXS locus.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream target to enhance precursor flux toward monoterpene production, though specific loci remain unresolved

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenolic monoterpene biosynthesis downstream of γ-terpinene/p-cymene
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyze oxidative transformations in the conversion of monoterpene precursors toward thymol and carvacrol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for downstream conversion toward target products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: 189 differentially expressed CYP genes identified; Tq06G018730.1 highlighted by qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with monoterpenoid-rich tissues and modules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate positive contributors to oxidation steps producing thymol and carvacrol from precursor monoterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The genome contains 420 CYP genes, with 189 CYP DEGs. 2. Phylogenetic analysis identified seven candidate CYP71D genes likely involved in phenolic monoterpene biosynthesis. 3. Authors state CYP71D genes are key genes related to thymol and carvacrol biosynthesis. 4. qRT-PCR supported an important role for Tq06G018730.1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; transcriptome DEG analysis; WGCNA; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key downstream oxidation step; not specifically rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Organ and floral developmental stage differences
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions are predicted from homology and expression; no direct enzyme assay in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; RNA-seq; WGCNA; volatile metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising downstream engineering targets to channel flux from γ-terpinene/p-cymene to thymol/carvacrol, especially combined with TqTPS1

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenolic monoterpene biosynthesis downstream of CYP-mediated oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyze redox conversions of oxidized monoterpene intermediates in thymol/carvacrol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for downstream conversion toward target products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: 47 SDR DEGs identified; Tq13G017990.1 highlighted by qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate positive contributors to final redox steps in thymol and carvacrol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The genome contains 163 SDR genes, including 47 SDR DEGs. 2. Phylogenetic analysis identified six candidate SDR genes likely involved in thymol/carvacrol biosynthesis. 3. Authors conclude SDR-encoding genes are key genes related to thymol and carvacrol biosynthesis. 4. qRT-PCR supported an important role for Tq13G017990.1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; transcriptome DEG analysis; WGCNA; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key downstream step; not specifically rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Organ and floral developmental stage differences
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted family-level assignment only; direct biochemical function not tested here.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; RNA-seq; WGCNA; volatile metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate downstream engineering targets for boosting phenolic monoterpene end products in combination with upstream TPS genes

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Glandular secretory trichome formation linked to monoterpenoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Potentially hormone-related trichome developmental signaling (general network context)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular secretory trichomes mainly in leaves and flowers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Differentially expressed; identified among candidate R2R3-MYBs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: qRT-PCR indicated important role in glandular secretory trichome formation and monoterpenoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes glandular secretory trichome development and indirectly supports monoterpenoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Phylogenetic analysis identified Tq12G014160.1 as a candidate R2R3-MYB involved in glandular secretory trichome development. 2. Transcriptome and WGCNA placed it in trichome/terpenoid-associated networks. 3. qRT-PCR supported its importance in monoterpenoid biosynthesis and glandular secretory trichome formation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; DEG heatmap; WGCNA; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate regulator linking trichome development with monoterpenoid production
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulatory target to increase glandular trichomes and indirectly enhance monoterpenoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Organ identity and flower developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct target genes or transactivation assays were reported.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Likely transcriptional regulation of trichome development
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Glandular secretory trichome formation, indirectly affecting monoterpenoid biosynthesis/storage
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Trichome developmental programs; possibly phytohormone signaling context
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular secretory trichomes in leaves and flowers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High expression particularly in leaves and flowers for Tq01G004800.1, Tq03G017440.1, Tq04G024290.1, Tq05G008130.1, Tq05G022030.1, Tq06G008250.1, Tq07G000520.1, Tq07G008780.1, and Tq10G005330.1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in tissues enriched for glandular secretory trichomes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promote glandular secretory trichome development and thereby support monoterpenoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ten HD-ZIP IV genes were identified as likely trichome-related candidates by phylogenetic analysis. 2. Many showed particularly high expression in leaves and flowers, where glandular secretory trichomes are abundant. 3. Tq05G008130.1 was further supported by qRT-PCR as playing an important role in monoterpenoid biosynthesis and glandular secretory trichome formation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; DEG heatmap; tissue expression profiling; WGCNA; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditioning trichome development likely controls the cellular capacity for monoterpenoid biosynthesis and storage
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulatory targets to increase glandular trichome density and improve essential oil yield
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific downstream targets were not functionally established in this study.
